patch.value: pm-mcp bin is dead on npm installs: main-module guard fails under symlinked argv[1] (silent exit 0, no JSON-RPC output)

Repro: printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-06-18","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"x","version":"1"}}}\n' | pm-mcp -> exit 0, EMPTY stdout/stderr. Same request piped to 'node dist/mcp/server.js' responds correctly (serverInfo 2026.6.12, 25 tools). Cause: src/mcp/server.ts:1173 'if (process.argv[1] && fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) === process.argv[1])' — npm bin entries are SYMLINKS (e.g. ~/.local/share/mise/installs/node/25.9.0/bin/pm-mcp -> ../lib/node_modules/@unbrained/pm-cli/dist/mcp/server.js); Node resolves import.meta.url to the realpath while argv[1] keeps the symlink path, so the guard never matches and the process exits 0 without serving. Minimal repro: ln -s dist/mcp/server.js /tmp/x && node /tmp/x -> no output. Fix: compare via fs.realpathSync(process.argv[1]) (wrapped in try/catch), e.g. realpath both sides before comparing. Affects every npm global/local install of the pm-mcp binary on Linux/macOS; silent exit 0 is the worst failure mode for MCP clients (looks like instant clean shutdown).

author: codex-sdk-cli-agent

patch.value: Fixed pm-mcp npm-bin symlink main-module detection; helper, e2e bin smoke, coverage, and manual temp-dir validation passed.
Fixed pm-mcp main-module detection by realpath-resolving symlinked npm bin argv paths.
npm-style symlinked pm-mcp starts the MCP stdio server and responds to initialize.
Integration and manual symlink smoke both returned serverInfo.name=pm-mcp.
